risks.ISSUE: Port collision — both expose WebSocket /terminal on port 3000. topology.html expects system.js; Xcontainerx expects its own server. Only one can bind. The other silently fails.
risks.MITIGATION: Assign Xcontainerx to port 3001 or 3002. Update Xcontainerx client to use new port. Ensure topology.html explicitly targets system.js:3000 only.

risks.ISSUE: No active database in consolidation target. Users/payments/commissions exist in SQLite (/c/bridgeos/bridgeos.db) AND PostgreSQL (bridge-postgres:5432) with separate schemas and overlapping entities. Data loss risk during merge.
risks.MITIGATION: Establish aoe-unified-final PostgreSQL database BEFORE consolidation. Export bridgeos SQLite → PostgreSQL. Reconcile user IDs across both databases. Use transaction rollback migration scripts.

risks.ISSUE: Hard dependency: topology.html requires 4 endpoints (/api/full, /api/audit, /api/economics + WebSocket /terminal) ALL on port 3000. If system.js crashes, entire topology UI fails with no fallback.
risks.MITIGATION: Cache /api/full responses. Implement exponential backoff for polling. Provide offline mode UI. Add explicit error toast to operator.

risks.ISSUE: Silent fallback when ainode unavailable. Returns stub response instead of error. Operators are unaware LLM is offline — production queries silently degrade to stubs.
risks.MITIGATION: Remove silent fallback. Return explicit 503 when ainode is down. Add circuit breaker. Monitor ainode health and alert operators. Implement LLM readiness check on gateway startup.

risks.ISSUE: Connection string hardcoded to Docker-specific hostname 'bridge-ai-os-aoe-dromedaries-db-1:5432'. Consolidation outside Docker breaks payment processing. No environment variable override.
risks.MITIGATION: Refactor to read DB_HOST from environment variable. Support both Docker internal DNS and standard hostnames. Test with local PostgreSQL before consolidation.

risks.ISSUE: Broken dashboard: calls localhost:4201/api/dci/ws and /api/dash-face/twin-state. No server exists on port 4201. Dashboard entirely non-functional.
risks.MITIGATION: Remove immersive.html from consolidation (recommended) OR implement missing port 4201 server. No supporting backend identified.

risks.ISSUE: 4+ dashboards read local JSON via file:// (nodes.json, mesh.json, kernel_state.json, etc.). Works in local dev only. Breaks in any consolidated/hosted/Docker deployment. Source of JSON is unknown.
risks.MITIGATION: Identify current JSON source (write-server unknown). Implement HTTP API endpoints for registry data. Migrate all file:// reads to fetch(). Remove file:// dependency entirely.

risks.ISSUE: Port 8080 claimed by: gateway.js (aoe-unified-final), BridgeAI mesh node, BRIDGE_AI_OS gateway, bridgeos gateway.js. Only one can bind.
risks.MITIGATION: Designate single gateway.js as the 8080 owner. Reassign or remove the others. Single Traefik/reverse-proxy in front is the cleanest solution per CONSOLIDATED_AUDIT.md.

risks.ISSUE: Complete control plane exists but has zero dashboard interface. Conflict detection, verification, optimization endpoints never called by any UI. Invisible to operators.
risks.MITIGATION: Either create dashboard UI for orchestrator status, or remove if unused. Document whether L1/L2/L3 is critical dependency.

risks.ISSUE: 8+ endpoints (/revenue/plans, /revenue/stats, /revenue/subscribe, /revenue/credits/purchase) exist on ainode but are never called by any dashboard. Orphaned functionality.
risks.MITIGATION: Audit whether revenue endpoints are used by external APIs or scripts. If unused, remove. If used, add dashboard UI or document integration points.

risks.ISSUE: Xcontainerx requires AUTH_TOKEN on WebSocket header. system.js has no auth. Incompatible terminal security models if both deployed.
risks.MITIGATION: Standardize terminal auth. Add token auth to system.js OR remove from Xcontainerx. Use secure environment variable (not hardcoded 'secure-token-change-me').

risks.ISSUE: Avatar connects to ws://localhost:8000 but BRIDGE_AI_OS FastAPI runs on port 8000 as HTTP (not a WebSocket terminal). Mismatch.
risks.MITIGATION: Clarify avatar's expected WebSocket server. Either add WebSocket support to FastAPI server or update avatar to use correct endpoint.

risks.ISSUE: mesh.json, nodes.json, kernel_state.json, telemetry.json, wallets.json, ledger.json polled every 2-5s by dashboards but NO server identified that writes them. State source completely unknown.
risks.MITIGATION: Audit file modification timestamps. Find the write source. If no source exists, data is stale/static — dashboards display meaningless data.

risks.ISSUE: PM2 config exists but conflicts with Docker-based consolidation. Multiple orchestration frameworks competing.
risks.MITIGATION: Choose one: PM2 (local dev) or Docker (consolidated production). Remove PM2 scripts if Docker consolidation chosen.

THEN_BREAKS: Xcontainerx terminal (port conflict — will also fail)
All HTTP health checks routed through gateway
Single point of failure for external routing
Real LLM responses degrade silently to stubs
Users receive low-quality AI responses without warning

safe_to_remove: /c/BridgeAI/immersive.html — broken, calls port 4201 which doesn't exist
/c/BridgeAI/registry/network_dashboard.html — file:// protocol only, won't work consolidated
Xcontainerx/server.js — IF system.js terminal is kept (choose one, not both)
PM2 orchestration scripts — IF Docker consolidation is chosen
/c/aoe-unified-final/ui.html — minimal 9-line stub, replace with real UI
Orphaned ainode revenue endpoints — IF confirmed unused by external systems

requires_migration.REASON: Hardcoded Docker hostname breaks if DB moves
requires_migration.ACTION: Externalize DB_HOST to environment variable. Test with local PostgreSQL.

requires_migration.REASON: Split across bridgeos SQLite and BridgeAI PostgreSQL with schema mismatch
requires_migration.ACTION: Establish unified PostgreSQL. Export+import SQLite data. Reconcile user IDs. Validate integrity.

requires_migration.REASON: system.js and Xcontainerx both claim port 3000 /terminal
requires_migration.ACTION: Choose ONE (system.js recommended). Assign other to port 3001. Update all client references.

requires_migration.REASON: file:// reads break in consolidated deployment
requires_migration.ACTION: Identify JSON write source. Implement HTTP API endpoints. Migrate all file:// reads.

requires_migration.REASON: Hides ainode service failure from operators
requires_migration.ACTION: Replace with explicit 503. Add circuit breaker and health checks.

requires_migration.REASON: Connects to ws://localhost:8000 but no WebSocket server on 8000
requires_migration.ACTION: Clarify intended WS server. Add WS support to FastAPI or update avatar endpoint.

consolidation_phases.NAME: Port Conflict Resolution
ACTIONS: Choose primary terminal: system.js (GOD MODE) or Xcontainerx
Assign secondary terminal to port 3001 or 3002
Update client code (topology.html, Xcontainerx/public/index.html)
Resolve port 8080 collision — designate single gateway owner
Verify port bindings with netstat before starting services
PREREQUISITES: Identify which terminal server is mission-critical

consolidation_phases.NAME: Database Consolidation
ACTIONS: Create aoe-unified-final PostgreSQL database (or adopt bridgedb)
Export bridgeos SQLite schema and data
Create migration scripts: SQLite → PostgreSQL with rollback
Reconcile user IDs across systems
Validate data integrity (count checks, constraint validation)
Update all DB connection strings
Test Xpayments:4000 and ainode:3001 connectivity

consolidation_phases.NAME: File-Based State Migration
ACTIONS: Identify source of registry JSON files (nodes.json, mesh.json, etc.)
Create HTTP API endpoints: GET /api/registry/nodes, /api/registry/mesh, etc.
Update all dashboards to fetch from HTTP instead of file://
Remove file:// protocol dependencies entirely
Establish single source of truth for registry state

consolidation_phases.NAME: Dashboard Cleanup
ACTIONS: Archive and remove broken/file-only dashboards to /deprecated/
Fix BRIDGE_AI_OS avatar WebSocket endpoint
Fix or remove immersive.html
Consolidate duplicate topology.html files
Consolidate duplicate system-status-dashboard.html files

consolidation_phases.NAME: Gateway & Failure Mode Fixes
ACTIONS: Remove silent fallback in gateway.js — explicit 503 when ainode unavailable
Add circuit breaker for ainode dependency
Add explicit error signaling to operators
Test all failure scenarios (ainode down, DB down)

consolidation_phases.NAME: Orchestrator Visibility
ACTIONS: Assess if L1/L2/L3 orchestrators are mission-critical
If critical: create dashboard UI for orchestrator status
If not: document and remove from consolidation
Expose conflict detection and optimization results to operators

consolidation_phases.NAME: Validation & Smoke Tests
ACTIONS: Verify no port collisions (netstat check)
Test DB connectivity from all services
Smoke test all critical dashboards
Verify WebSocket connections (/terminal, /events)
Test payment webhook end-to-end
Monitor error logs for 24 hours

consolidation_phases.NAME: Cutover & Rollback Readiness
ACTIONS: Create full backup before cutover
Execute consolidated system startup per CONSOLIDATED_AUDIT.md
Monitor for 24-48 hours
Keep legacy running alongside until confidence reached
Document and test rollback procedure

summary.estimated_effort: 4-6 weeks: Phase 1 port resolution (3 days), Phase 2 DB consolidation (5-7 days), Phase 3 state migration (3-5 days), Phase 4 dashboard cleanup (2-3 days), Phase 5 gateway fixes (2 days), Phase 6 orchestrator (3 days), Phase 7 validation (5-7 days), Phase 8 cutover (5 days). Phases 3-6 can run in parallel. Total: ~25-35 work days.
top_3_blockers: BLOCKER 1 (Port Collision): system.js vs Xcontainerx BOTH on port 3000. Must choose one and reassign before ANY dashboard testing works.
BLOCKER 2 (No Database): aoe-unified-final has no active database. Must establish unified PostgreSQL and migrate SQLite data BEFORE moving payment/auth systems.

BLOCKER 3 (Unknown State Source): registry JSON files (nodes.json, mesh.json, etc.) are polled by dashboards but NO server writes them. Must identify source or dashboards display stale/meaningless data.

consolidation_readiness.aoe-unified-final: NOT READY — No database, port conflicts, broken dashboards, missing state infrastructure
consolidation_readiness.bridgeos: PARTIAL — SQLite database exists, gateway has silent fallback flaw, PM2 orchestration works
consolidation_readiness.BridgeAI: LEGACY — File-based registry, broken immersive.html, multiple orphaned dashboards, ainode is functional
consolidation_readiness.BRIDGE_AI_OS: PARTIAL — Audit plan exists (CONSOLIDATED_AUDIT.md), Docker setup partially complete
consolidation_readiness.OVERALL: NOT READY FOR CONSOLIDATION — Resolve 3 blockers first
